target_keywords: colorado workers comp right of control

sources.citation: CRS § 8-70-115 (Colorado Employment Security Act — worker classification for UI)
CRS § 8-12-106 (wilful misclassification civil penalty: up to $5,000 per worker first offence; up to $25,000 per worker repeat offence)
HB 09-1310 (2009) — added substantial-consistency requirement to safe-harbour
SB 21-87 (2021) — Agricultural Workers' Rights Act; CDLE Misclassification Advisory Opinion authority
HB 22-1117 (2022) — Workplace Accountability for Misclassification; private right of action; expanded CDLE subpoena power
IRS Rev. Rul. 87-41, 1987-1 C.B. 296 (20-factor common-law test)
US DOL NPRM RIN 1235-AA46 (FLSA independent contractor classification, NPRM 27 Feb 2026)
Colorado Department of Labor and Employment (CDLE) — Worker Classification Guide
Colorado Division of Workers' Compensation — Independent Contractor Guidance

high_concern_flags.detail: Penalty schedule (up to $5,000 first offence / up to $25,000 repeat offence) drawn from secondary sources aligned with statute text. CRS primary text confirms 'up to' caps, not fixed amounts. Page text uses 'up to' throughout to match statutory language.

spec_notes: Modelled on CA / AZ sibling structure with Colorado-specific data swaps from sprint/compliance-cache/colorado.json.
Default test is IRS common-law, NOT a state ABC test (distinguished explicitly from CA AB5).
Nine-factor safe-harbour at CRS § 8-70-115(1)(c) is the practical compliance lever; substantial-consistency requirement from HB 09-1310 (2009) emphasised.
Workers' comp track is separate: CRS § 8-40-202(2) right-of-control + § 8-40-202(2)(b)(IV) parallel safe-harbour.
SB 21-87 (2021) and HB 22-1117 (2022) discussed in own section: HB 22-1117 added private right of action plus expanded CDLE subpoena power.
CRS § 8-12-106 wilful-misclassification civil penalty bands noted as 'up to' to match statutory language.
FAMLI back-premium called out as distinctive Colorado misclassification exposure not on the federal checklist.
Hero photo reused from sibling state-income-tax-and-unemployment-insurance page (same Denver skyline, same photographer credit).
